expectations.text: Output file is a duplicate, original 12-health-wellness.html is untouched
expectations.evidence: Output is 12-health-wellness-animated.html (533 lines) in the outputs directory. The original 12-health-wellness.html (235 lines) remains at its source path with no GSAP scripts, no animation classes (.hero-section, .reveal-card, etc.), and still has 'card-lift' classes (vs 'reveal-card' in animated version) and original CSS transitions (.btn-sage { transition: background-color 0.2s ease; }) that were removed in the animated version. The two files are clearly different.

expectations.text: GSAP and ScrollTrigger CDN scripts are included
expectations.evidence: Lines 29-31 of the output: <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/gsap@3.14/dist/gsap.min.js"></script>, <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/gsap@3.14/dist/ScrollTrigger.min.js"></script>, <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/gsap@3.14/dist/SplitText.min.js"></script>. Both GSAP core and ScrollTrigger are present (plus SplitText bonus).

expectations.text: Motion personality is 'subtle' — durations bias toward base/slow (0.6-1.0s), not micro/fast
expectations.evidence: Main content animation durations: hero SplitText 1.0s, hero badge 0.7s, hero body 0.8s, hero CTAs 0.7s, hero metrics 0.8s, hero image 1.2s, section labels 0.7s, section headings 0.8s, section body 0.7s, card reveals 0.7s, footer 0.8s. All primary scroll/entrance animations are 0.7-1.2s, firmly in the base-slow range. Only navbar (0.4s) and hover micro-interactions (0.4-0.5s) use shorter durations, which is appropriate. Transcript confirms personality set to 'subtle' with explicit adaptation table showing base-slow bias.

expectations.text: Easing uses entrance (power2.out) or transition (power2.inOut), NOT spring or elastic
expectations.evidence: Every easing value in the animation code is 'power2.out' (entrance) or 'power2.in' (navbar exit only) or 'none' (parallax scrub). Zero occurrences of 'spring', 'elastic', 'back.out', or 'bounce' in easing properties. The code at line 458 explicitly notes: 'ease: "power2.out" // token: entrance (NOT spring — calm brand)'.

expectations.text: Hero section has parallax effect (yPercent with scrub:true) gated to desktop only
expectations.evidence: Lines 326-338: Touch detection via 'const isTouch = "ontouchstart" in window || navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0;' then 'if (!isTouch)' guards the parallax. Inside: gsap.to('.hero-image', { yPercent: -8, ease: 'none', scrollTrigger: { trigger: '.hero-section', start: 'top top', end: 'bottom top', scrub: 1.5 } }). Has yPercent and scrub, gated to non-touch (desktop) devices.

expectations.text: Ritual cards have staggered scroll reveal animation
expectations.evidence: Lines 417-443: Cards are selected via '.reveal-card', grouped by parent section via card.closest('section'). The ritual section (#rituals) contains 3 cards with class 'reveal-card'. Each card gets gsap.fromTo with autoAlpha:0->1, y:30->0, delay: i * 0.13 (stagger), and ScrollTrigger with start: 'top 88%'. The ritual section's 3 cards are iterated with i=0,1,2 producing delays of 0, 0.13, 0.26s.

expectations.text: Product cards have staggered scroll reveal animation
expectations.evidence: Same code block (lines 417-443) groups cards by parent section. The products section (#products) contains 4 cards with class 'reveal-card'. Each gets the same staggered reveal (delay: i * 0.13) with ScrollTrigger. Product cards are iterated with i=0,1,2,3 producing delays of 0, 0.13, 0.26, 0.39s.

expectations.text: Journal section has scroll-triggered entrance animation
expectations.evidence: The journal section (#journal) has: (1) section-label and section-heading classes on its label/heading (lines 172-174), which are animated by the section reveal code at lines 364-394 with ScrollTrigger. (2) Three journal cards with class 'reveal-card' (lines 179, 187, 195) are animated by the card stagger code at lines 417-443 with ScrollTrigger. Both the heading and cards have scroll-triggered entrance animations.

expectations.text: gsap.matchMedia wraps all animation code with reduced-motion guard
expectations.evidence: Line 251: 'const mm = gsap.matchMedia();'. Line 254: 'mm.add("(prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)", () => {' wraps ALL animation code (lines 254-520). Line 523: 'mm.add("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)", () => {' provides the fallback that sets clearProps:'all' on all animated elements. Every animation call is inside the matchMedia no-preference block.

expectations.text: autoAlpha used instead of bare opacity in animation calls
expectations.evidence: Grep for bare 'opacity' in the output HTML returned zero matches. All fade animations use 'autoAlpha': hero badge (autoAlpha:0->1), hero body (autoAlpha:0->1), hero CTAs (autoAlpha:0->1), hero metrics (autoAlpha:0->1), hero image (autoAlpha:0->1), SplitText words (autoAlpha:0), section labels/headings/body (autoAlpha:0->1), card reveals (autoAlpha:0->1), footer (autoAlpha:0->1). No bare 'opacity' in any gsap call.

expectations.text: No layout-triggering properties animated (no width, height, top, left, margin)
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'width|height|top:|left:|margin' returned only: viewport meta tag (line 5) and getBoundingClientRect() reading (lines 481-483, reading position, not animating). No gsap.to/fromTo/from call animates width, height, top, left, or margin. Only transform-based properties are animated: y, yPercent, scale, x (via quickTo for magnetic), and autoAlpha (opacity+visibility).

expectations.text: Token comments present in animation values
expectations.evidence: 29 instances of '// token:' comments found across the animation code. Examples: 'duration: 1.0, // token: slow (breathing pace)', 'ease: "power2.out", // token: entrance (gentle)', 'delay: i * 0.13, // token: loose stagger (unhurried)', 'duration: 0.4, // token: fast / exit', 'duration: 0.8, // token: base+'. Every animation call has at least one token comment documenting the design intent.

claims.evidence: The corrected transcript lists 9 numbered patterns under 'Patterns Implemented (9 total)', and metrics.json now reports patterns_applied: 9. All 9 patterns are present in the output HTML code.

claims.claim: SplitText with autoSplit and aria:true used for hero heading
claims.evidence: Lines 265-270: new SplitText(heroH1, { type: 'lines,words', mask: 'lines', autoSplit: true, aria: true, onSplit(self) { buildHeroSplit(self); } }). Both autoSplit and aria:true are present.

claims.claim: CSS transitions removed to avoid GSAP conflict
claims.evidence: Line 21 in output: '.btn-sage { /* transition removed — GSAP owns hover */ }' — the original had '.btn-sage { transition: background-color 0.2s ease; }'. The transition was correctly removed.

Transcript Phase 3 shows 14 [PASS] checks. The output HTML code is consistent with all claimed checks (hero timeline has 5 chained fromTo, ScrollTrigger on all sections, interactive effects present, matchMedia wraps all code, ScrollTrigger.refresh on load, autoAlpha everywhere, no CSS transition conflicts, no deny-list properties, cleanup function present, touch guards, registerPlugin present, fromTo used throughout, CDN pinned to 3.14, reduced-motion fallback present).

claims.claim: Personality was adapted from default 'energetic' to 'subtle' for wellness brand
claims.evidence: The adaptation table in transcript shows concrete changes: y offsets reduced (40->15-30), durations lengthened (0.6->0.7-1.0), easing softened (power3.out->power2.out), stagger loosened (0.09->0.13), hover restrained (y:-8->y:-5, scale:1.02->1.015), parallax reduced (yPercent:-20->-8). All values verified in output HTML.

suggestions.assertion: Hero section has parallax effect (yPercent with scrub:true) gated to desktop only
suggestions.reason: The gating uses touch detection ('ontouchstart' in window) rather than a matchMedia desktop breakpoint. This is reasonable but means a laptop with a touchscreen would not get parallax. The assertion says 'desktop only' — consider whether touch-detection vs. breakpoint-based gating matters for grading.

No assertion checks that the hero entrance timeline has chained .fromTo calls (the transcript claims 5+). This is a key quality signal for GSAP-native animation vs CSS-fallback behavior. Consider adding: 'Hero entrance uses a gsap.timeline() with at least 4 chained .fromTo calls'.
No assertion checks for ScrollTrigger.refresh() on window load. This is a critical correctness requirement (prevents misaligned scroll triggers) that the skill claims to enforce.

suggestions.assertion: Motion personality is 'subtle' — durations bias toward base/slow (0.6-1.0s), not micro/fast
suggestions.reason: One duration (hero image at 1.2s) exceeds the stated 0.6-1.0s range. The assertion still passes because the 'bias' language is correct — the bulk of durations are in range. But a stricter version could check that no primary animation exceeds 1.0s.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong eval set covering the key motion-dev quality gates. The 12 assertions are discriminating — they check GSAP-specific patterns (autoAlpha, matchMedia, token comments) that a CSS-only or naive implementation would fail. Minor gaps: no assertion on timeline chaining depth or ScrollTrigger.refresh().
